Description of support provided
Professor McWilliams is a Professor of Critical Care and Rehabilitation within University Hospitals Coventry and
Warwickshire NHS Trust and Coventry University. He has extensive clinical experience in critical care
physiotherapy and complex rehabilitation. He is Chair of the Intensive Care Society National Rehabilitation
Collabor
sectdars__001_k.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_l.text:
ative and has contributed to the development of national guidance for critical illness rehabilitation (NICE
CG83, NICE QS158). David’s research interests focus on rehabilitation and recovery following a period of critical
illness, with >50 peer reviewed publications and over £5 million in grant income as lead applicant or within
research teams.
David is a recognised international expert in critical care physiotherapy and rehabilitation, regularly presenting
nationally and internationally. David actively supports and mentors clinical academic AHPs from across the UK,
including 2 current NIHR DCAF students and 2 NIHR PCAF awards. David will provide practice supervision
specifically around the development and evaluation of complex rehabilitation interventions within an NHS context.
As an established Senior Clinical Academic and Clinical Professor, he will also provide direction and mentorship
on the development of a senior clinical academic role.
With an established supervisory relationship that started over one year ago, David has a thorough understanding
of my professional training and development needs, where we have met individually or with my academic
supervisors every 2-3 months
sectdars__001_l.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_m.text:
. Alongside our research collaboration, he will also use his experience to provide
direct support and mentorship in my development towards a clinical academic role. We will meet online bi-monthly
to discuss my progress.
PRACTICE SUPERVISOR 2
Name Dr Hatem Soliman
Institution The Royal Brompton and Harefield Hospitals
Position Consultant Intensivist in Cardiothoracic Intensive Care
Description of support provided
Dr Soliman is a Consultant in Cardiothoracic Intensive Care at Royal Brompton and Harefield Hospitals, visiting
Professor at Mayo Clinic, Florida and Honorary Senior Lecturer at the School of Cardiovascular Sciences and
Medicine at King's College London. Dr Soliman has >25 peer reviewed publications in point of care ultrasound in
ICU and is Associate Editor of the Critical Care Ultrasound Journal and Frontiers in Cardiovascular Imaging. He is
lead editor of a textbook entitled ‘Cardiopulmonary Point of Care Ultrasound’, published in 2023.
Dr Soliman has an international profile in teaching critical care ultrasound and was awarded the prestigious
excellence in education awards from the European association of cardiovascular imaging in 2022.
Dr Soliman has trained >200 medics
sectdars__001_m.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_n.text:
, six physiotherapists and nine nurses in Focused Ultrasound in Intensive
Care (FUSIC) internationally. He developed a multidisciplinary point of care ultrasound training course where
diaphragm ultrasound is a core part of the programme. The programme was shortlisted for the best new
educational programme in the Intensive Care Society awards 2024.
Dr Soliman will provide extensive teaching and supervision to refine my skills in diaphragm ultrasound before
study 2 commences. This will include four hours per week (over three months) of combined and independent
diaphragm scanning on mechanically ventilated patients. Dr Soliman and I have an established relationship (>4
years), where he has previously supervised me in conducting lung and diaphragm ultrasound. Dr Soliman will
provide support throughout the fellowship with bi-monthly supervisions and additional support as required during
study 2.
Collaborations
Royal Brompton and Harefield Hospitals PPI team
In addition to extensive ongoing and planned PPI involvement in the proposed project, I will continue to collaborate
with the PPI lead at the Trust, Zahra Aden, for ongoing advice and support. With this, I will present findings to th
sectdars__001_n.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_o.text:
e
established respiratory PPI group at the Royal Brompton and Harefield Hospitals (RBHT) and gain opinions and
advice on any further dissemination strategies they might suggest.
Post-graduate education and supervision, Brunel University
I understand a career as a clinical academic leader means contribution towards the training and development of
both future clinical academics and clinicians. My experience teaching on MSc modules, supporting two MSc
students, and supporting clinicians with research has begun this process. An important element of my
development as a clinical academic leader will be for me to support further MSc projects; I will collaborate with my
academic supervisory team and other research leaders at Brunel university to facilitate this. The number of
projects I support will be discussed with my supervisory team to ensure it does not impact on timescales within this
proposed application. I will also take further opportunities to teach on appropriate MSc advanced clinical practice
modules.
Lee Bolton, clinical lead, speech and language therapist, RBHT ICU
I will continue my collaboration with Lee Bolton who will offer support and advice before and during study 1. Le
sectdars__001_o.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_p.text:
e
Bolton has extensive expertise in the assessment and management of communication critical care. This includes
patients with tracheostomy and ventilator-dependency who have concomitant laryngeal and upper airway
complications secondary to critical care interventions. He also lectures on critical care postgraduate modules for
Brunel University and University of West London.
In collaboration with the projects PPI group, his specialist input will support development of the written
communication boards for study 1 of the proposed research. He will provide expert advice and treatment for
complex patients recruited to study 1, to ensure participants are able to communicate as effectively as possible for
the qualitative data collection proposed.
International Collaborations
Professor Stephen Rees and Associate Professor Dan Karbing
I will continue my collaboration with Professor Rees and Associate Professor Karbing who head the Respiratory
and Critical Care group at Aalborg University, Denmark. They are experts in the physiological response to exercise
and ventilatory weaning in mechanically ventilated patients. They have published lab data on diaphragmatic
activation measured by electro
sectdars__001_p.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_q.text:
myography (EMG) during exercise, and respiratory muscle fatigue during loaded
breathing. They are co-authors on my published work and one manuscript under review. I am visiting them in
November 2024 for a research visit as part of my PCAF to analyse lab data collected during my charity fellowship.
Professor Karbing has provided advice on the healthy EMG IMT study at Brunel (results of which inform this
DCAF) and on elements of the proposed work in this fellowship. Our continued collaboration during my DCAF via
video conferencing and email will ensure journal outputs for our healthy IMT data and potential post-doctoral
research collaborations.
Associate Professor Ulrike Pielmeier
I will continue a growing collaboration with Associate Professor Ulrike Pielmeier in the Department of Health
Science and Technology at Aalborg University, where I was initially approached to advise on an early mobilisation
system in critical care. We now plan to conduct a short, one month study at Harefield Hospital in January 2025,
measuring agreement of an early mobilisation decision system versus clinician decision to mobilise in critical care.
This work will be concluded before the proposed start date
sectdars__001_q.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_r.text:
of my DCAF. Results from this study may inform
subsequent studies in my post-doctoral career, and further strengthens links with Aalborg University.
Host Organisation Support Statement
The following statement of support should be composed jointly by the Heads of Department / Senior Managers of
the employing host organisation and the partner organisation.
The Heads of Department / Senior Managers of these organisations will be able to add the statement of support to
the form once they have confirmed participation. Invitations to participate are sent by the applicant via the
'Participants and Signatories' section of the form.
Statement of support
Tim has been at Royal Brompton and Harefield hospitals (RBHH) for six years and is already recognised as a
potential future leader in research. During this time, he has completed a ten-month competitively awarded Royal
Brompton and Harefield Hospitals Charity (RBHC) Fellowship in Inspiratory Muscle Training (IMT), which is Tim’s
area of interest for this DCAF. Tim recruited his planned sample size in record time and was able to then double
his recruitment target. He used the RBHC Fellowship to establish links with key collaborators in the UK
sectdars__001_r.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_s.text:
and
abroad who will be integral to his research in the future. Tim was also personally selected to take part in an
Imperial College London ‘Starting Out in Research’ course, which he now mentors on. He is currently completing
an NIHR PCAF which is the foundation for this DCAF.
Royal Brompton and Harefield Hospitals (RBHH) have a long-standing successful partnership with Brunel
University London (Brunel), having joined the Brunel Partners Academic Centre for Health Sciences (BPACHS),
and we believe we offer the ideal environment for Tim to advance as a practitioner academic. As a partnership,
we also support a lecturer (Lewis) on an ongoing NIHR Research for Patient Benefit grant. Our links are further
strengthened through Dr Claire Nolan who is completing an NIHR AF across our organisations.
Clinically, Tim will be hosted by the Rehabilitation and Therapies (R&T) Directorate of RBHH. Tim's application
comes with strong support from the Directorate and the Trust as it aligns with our aim to develop NMAHPP-led
research. The R&T Directorate currently has a significant number of ongoing fellowship recipients and we have
been able to facilitate these through successful backfill. We val
sectdars__001_s.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_t.text:
ue our fellows and Tim has full support from the
critical care therapy management team, and the R&T team, to maintain clinical practice and progress as one of our
future practitioner academic leaders.
RBHH have an active and excelling allied-health professional (AHP) community with regular AHP-focused for a,
giving our researchers an opportunity to come together to discuss research and offer peer-support and fellowship
guidance to one another. As a physiotherapist, Tim will be well supported by an expanding team of independent
researchers in the same profession who have completed PCAFs (x2) and/or are undertaking or have recently
completed NIHR DCAFs/CDRFs (x6), and those who have secured and are leading on their own research grants
as Principal Investigators (x4). Two of Tim’s peer group (Grillo and Balls) have successfully moved within the
NIHR ICA pathway from PCAF to DCAF, as Tim hopes to, and will be an invaluable support to him on his
practitioner academic journey.
Academically, Tim will be hosted within the Department of Health Sciences at Brunel. The department has a
strong focus on interdisciplinary research and inter-professional learning, hosting multi-professional disci
sectdars__001_t.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_u.text:
plines
(Physiotherapy, Occupational therapy, Nursing, Social Work, Public Health and Health Economics). Tim will have
access to eight different Research Centres and will specifically be attached to the Centre for Physical Activity in
Health and Disease. The department is supported by a dedicated Director of Postgraduate Research, Dr Wendy
Martin, who promotes excellence in doctoral education, training and engagement. Tim will benefit from the
departments’ prioritisation of a values-based approach to doctoral research which ensures a positive and ethical
organisational culture that facilitates academic citizenship. Tim will be further supported by the Graduate School
through various training programmes and will have a dedicated advisor who will monitor and support his career
development.
Brunel’s Physiotherapy Division has a large number of research-active academics working across a range of
clinical and laboratory-based topics and has a wealth of qualitative, quantitative and mixed-methods experience.
We hold a monthly divisional research meeting, at which doctoral researchers are actively encouraged to
participate, presenting study plans, progress, and research findings in a frien
sectdars__001_u.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_v.text:
dly and supportive environment.
These meetings are also an opportunity to gain exposure to broader research topics, supporting Tim’s
development as a well-rounded practitioner academic. Tim has already been a valuable contributor as a guest
lecturer on our Advanced Clinical Practice MSc programme; he will have the opportunity to further develop his
teaching practice during his doctoral studies. This may include assisting in supervision of MSc research projects.
The Department has a commitment to research excellence and was highly evaluated on the Research Excellence
Framework (REF) 2021. In the Unit of Assessment 3 (Allied Health Professions, Dentistry, Nursing and Pharmacy)
84% outputs were rated 3* OR 4*, with 42% as 4* (“World leading”), 75% of impact case studies were rated at 3*
or 4*and 100% of the research environment was rated as 3 or 4*. Tim will therefore be hosted and supported by a
department that is research-intensive and progressive in designing, conducting and translating research into
clinical practice and wider policies.
As one of the largest employers in the NHS, GSTT (of which RBHH is part) has an incredibly diverse workforce
and takes proactive measures at all l
sectdars__001_v.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_w.text:
evels to make equality, diversity, and inclusion (EDI) a part of everyday
business. A Positive Action Charter has been established for managers and senior leaders to pledge their
commitment to helping GSTT embed a culture of EDI across the organisation. Other EDI initiatives, summarised
on an EDI toolkit, include inclusion agents, rainbow badges, sunflower lanyards, staff health passports, workshops,
Positive Pathways development programme, mandatory EDI training and many more staff networks.
Brunel is a research intensive and civic university, renowned for its commitment to diversity, to widening
participation and with a social mission to make a positive and meaningful difference to people’s lives and the
environment, locally and globally (see https://www.staff.brunel.ac.uk/documents/Wielding-Brunels-World-class-
Difference.pdf). Tim’s host department achieved an Athena Silver Award in 2023, demonstrating commitment to
gender equality in education and research. Brunel research processes are guided by the EDI principles, supported
by a dedicated Associate Dean for EDI, Dr Mike Thomas, a social work researcher.
With our existing and developing links, RBHH and Brunel are committed to
sectdars__001_w.source_path: APPLICATION DETAILS > Training & Development and Research Support

sectdars__001_x.text:
ensuring that Tim receives the required
time, support, training and networking opportunities to allow him to successfully complete his DCAF objectives.
